note: Occulted flare on NE limb from the newly numbered AR 14384 rotating on disk, so the location of the AR/flare is somewhat uncertain.
End time is cut off by a small, but visually distinct in GOES SUVI 131, separate C3.2 peak from AR 14401 (N23E65) starting at 2026-03-23T00:24Z that peaks at 2026-03-23T00:29Z.
Double-peaked flare preceding a C3.7 flare peaking at 2026-03-25T00:30Z.
GOES-P (GOES-18) eclipse period during this flare, GOES-S used for entry.

Concurrent brightening with an unnumbered region around the east limb ~N06. That area brightens alongside AR 14423, with AR 14423 taking the peak brightness at 2026-04-24T13:01Z, unable to cleanly separate the brightenings in the GOES-P X-ray flux timeline.
There is a simultaneous, longer duration significant flare from Active region 14425 (N06E52) contributing to this event.

Eruption from an active region located around the east limb at time of eruption, now numbered as AR 14436. Approximate location around E110-E100 as observed from Solar Orbiter EUI 195 at 2026-05-07T00:01Z and rotation of the sun since that image was taken.
Flare may be located slightly beyond the west limb, W90-W95.
Flare may be slightly occulted by the limb, AR 14435 located around N22W91 at this time.
First peak on a double-peaked flare from two separate regions of the disk, second peak of C4.0 at 2026-06-05T15:13Z.
Second peak on a double-peaked flare from two separate regions of the disk, first peak of C4.1 at 2026-06-05T15:03Z.
